fedEvents.content: QXO is taking its offer directly to shareholders after being rebuffed on several occasions.

fedEvents.content: The personal consumption expenditures price index was expected to show a 4.1% annual increase.

items.title: Apple’s MacBooks and iPads are getting more expensive as the memory crunch deepens
items.content: Apple increased prices on select MacBooks and iPads Thursday morning on the heels of a blowout Micron earnings report.

items.title: My son passed away. Am I crazy to move to my former home and sell a house I bought in September?
items.content: “I raised my children there as a single mother, and it was the last home my son lived in before he passed away unexpectedly.”

items.title: A $600,000 Ferrari? How healthcare scam artists are laughing at the rest of us.
items.content: Ordinary people are trying to make the rent, and fraudsters are ripping off Medicare, Medicaid and private insurance.

items.title: Consumer spending rises again — and now cheaper gas is on the way
items.content: Americans have been spending more, but mostly because of rising inflation.

items.title: ‘I’m scared of making a huge financial mistake’: I gave my brother half of my $1.5 million home. Can he force me to sell?
items.content: “One lawyer estimated that my brother and I would each walk away with a couple hundred thousand dollars.”

items.title: Rocket Lab, Lockheed Martin among the partners in SpaceX’s military space-laser project
items.content: Government documents show a lineup of defense contractors joining SpaceX to create a satellite network to track airborne threats.

items.title: I had gallbladder surgery. After I got home, the hospital asked me for a financial donation. Is this ethical?
items.content: “The letter asked whether I had a favorite caregiver and if I would like to make a contribution in their honor.”

items.title: These S&P 500 stocks are expected to make double-digit comebacks

items.title: Here are five themes Wall Street left behind while chasing a single trade

items.title: Could the U.S. join Germany in raising its retirement age?
items.content: Germany may gradually raise its retirement age to 70 by 2092 — but such a move would fix only part of Social Security’s funding gap.

items.title: Stocks Sink in Broad AI Rout Sparked by China's DeepSeek
items.content: U.S. stocks were mostly lower, with the Nasdaq leading declines as makers of AI infrastructure suffered steep falls, many in the double digits. Nvidia was down 16%.

items.title: Swiss franc, Japanese yen Rise as DeepSeek News Boosts Safe Havens
items.content: The Yen and Swiss Franc were stronger against the dollar as investors sought safe havens after Chinese start-up DeepSeek’s new AI model hit U.S. tech stocks.

items.title: Russia's Crude Exports Fairly Stable Despite U.S. Sanctions, Goldman Says

items.title: TikTok and YouTube are reinventing sports viewership. Broadcasters are taking note

items.title: Inside Wealth: Luxury spending now driven by experiences and 'inheritourism'

items.title: Iraq piles pressure on OPEC over quota dispute after UAE exit

items.title: Darden Restaurants earnings beat estimates but Olive Garden growth weakens

items.title: Amazon adds new funding, lifting India AI and cloud investment to $48 billion

items.title: Despite Trump trade rhetoric, Asian investors keep betting on U.S.: Former House Majority Leader Eric Cantor

items.title: Trump meets defense CEOs as Iran operations strain U.S. missile stockpiles
